title: Earnings call transcript: Enovix Q1 2026 beats forecasts, stock rises
text: Enovix Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights. Financial Performance: The company reported revenue of $7.6 million, surpassing forecasts by 9.35%. Non-GAAP earnings per share came in at -$0.14, better than the anticipated -$0.16. The company achieved a non-GAAP gross margin of 26.3%, marking the sixth consecutive quarter of positive gross profit. Revenue grew 49% year-over-year, primarily driven by repeat orders from Korean military contractors. Smart Eyewear Platform: The company commenced commercial production of its AI-1 battery for a lead customer's reference platform, with initial shipments underway. Management projects smart eyewear could represent a $1 billion market opportunity by decade's end. The newly developed AI-2 platform delivers greater than 20% higher volumetric energy density compared to AI-1. Smartphone Qualification Progress: Enovix aligned with Honor on revised qualification standards that prioritize real-world usage testing over accelerated stress protocols. Management noted they removed the 0.7C discharge requirement as a gating item, focusing instead on 0.2C testing that better reflects real-world usage conditions. Defense & Drone Opportunities: The company launched MX1-B01, a drone battery achieving 360 Wh/kg energy density. The global pipeline for Korean-manufactured products now exceeds $130 million, with drones representing over 60% of this opportunity.
